mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near the lower bound and continues to fall, discount-rate pressure eases faster than earnings expectations deteriorate, lifting equity prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is also decelerating, because inflation relief reduces the chance of an imminent policy reversal.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls for 2 consecutive monthly observations
regime_hint: policy easing near the effective lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show positive 20d SPX returns above baseline, or if the sign turns negative when CPIAUCSL is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, which leads analysts to cut earnings forecasts and traders to price slower revenue growth. This should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when unemployment is rising from an already elevated level.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if rising-UNRATE trigger windows do not produce negative 20d returns more often than not, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly growth in RSAFS signals resilient consumer demand and better near-term revenue realization for large-cap firms, which supports positive equity returns over a one-month horizon. The effect should weaken if unemployment is simultaneously surging, because demand strength then reflects temporary inventory or price effects rather than durable spending.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive RSAFS increases fail to predict higher 20d SPX returns, or if the sign disappears when UNRATE is flat or rising.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO after a prior contraction indicates manufacturing stabilization and better capacity utilization, which improves cyclical earnings sentiment and lifts stocks over the next quarter. This effect should be strongest when housing is also rebounding, because the recovery is then broad-based rather than isolated to inventories.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 3 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if industrial upturn windows do not deliver positive 60d SPX returns, or if the signal vanishes when HOUST remains weak.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in FEDFUNDS after a tightening peak signals imminent policy accommodation, which raises the present value of future cash flows and supports equity prices over the next week. The effect fails when inflation is accelerating, because higher CPIAUCSL can prevent markets from treating lower rates as durable easing.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 50 bps from a recent 3-month peak
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive after rate cuts from a peak, or if the signal is null/negative when CPIAUCSL is rising on a 3-month basis.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS while UNRATE rises captures labor-market deterioration that transmits quickly into weaker consumer spending and lower earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is most reliable when both variables move in the same adverse direction rather than independently.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises in the same monthly release pair
failure_condition: Reject if joint deterioration episodes do not underperform baseline on 20d SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the sign better than the combined condition.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST after a prior trough signals renewed housing demand, better construction activity, and improved cyclical sentiment, which should support equity returns over the next quarter. The mechanism weakens if retail sales are simultaneously contracting, because housing strength alone then reflects rate effects rather than broad demand recovery.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases for 2 consecutive monthly observations from a 6-month low
failure_condition: Reject if housing-rebound episodes fail to show positive 60d SPX returns, or if the signal is absent when RSAFS is also improving.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained improvement in INDPRO combined with a stable or falling UNRATE indicates genuine real-side expansion rather than inflationary overheating, which should raise forward equity returns over one month. The signal flips off if industrial growth occurs while unemployment rises, because that pattern suggests inventory restocking without durable demand.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising INDPRO and stable/falling UNRATE do not outperform on 20d SPX returns, or if the relation reverses when CPIAUCSL is accelerating.
